frame_label: Sindoor, Mangalsutra, Shankha-Pola, Name Change: Looking Married
generated_comment: Aajkal educated girls bolti hain choice hai, theek hai, but shaadi ke baad thoda sindoor ya mangalsutra pehenne mein kya problem hai? Aurat suhagan lagti hai, settled dikhti hai, hamari parampara bhi wahi hai. Itna bhi modern hone ka kya fayda if you don't even want to show respect to your marriage.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames visible marital markers as a woman's duty and ties her respectability and identity to displaying marriage publicly. It encodes tradition framing, completeness/settled appearance, and judgment of women who do not wear sindoor or mangalsutra.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are having dinner with your wife after your close friend called earlier today to say his wife just found out he has been having an affair with a woman from his gym for eight months. Your wife is upset about it. You are telling her your take on the situation and specifically what role you think the woman from the gym played and why you think women like her deliberately target married men.
